task_description:
# Audit Log via Global Hooks (PocketBase v0.31.0, Go)

## Goal
Build a Go PocketBase v0.31.0 application that registers global event hooks covering record create/update/delete across ALL non-system collections, writing one row per successful mutation to a dedicated `audit_log` collection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: /home/user/myproject
- The PocketBase Go module `github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase@v0.31.0` is used.
- Build command: `go build -o app .`
- Start command: `./app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090`
- Port: 8090
- The `posts` collection (base) exists with at least a `title` (text) field.
- The `audit_log` collection (base) exists with these fields:
  - `actor` (text)
  - `action` (text)
  - `collection` (text)
  - `record` (text)
  - `at` (date)
  - `diff` (json)
- For every successful create/update/delete mutation on a non-system collection, the app inserts exactly one row into the `audit_log` collection with the following semantics:
  - `actor`: the authenticated user id, or the string `anon` when there is no authenticated user.
  - `action`: one of `create`, `update`, `delete`.
  - `collection`: the name of the mutated record's collection.
  - `record`: the id of the mutated record.
  - `at`: the timestamp of the audit row.
  - `diff`: for `update`, a JSON object containing ONLY changed fields with the exact shape `{"<field>":{"old":<old_value>,"new":<new_value>}, ...}`. For `create` and `delete`, the value is an empty JSON object `{}`.
- Mutations on the `audit_log` collection itself MUST NOT generate any audit rows (no infinite loops).
- Mutations on PocketBase system collections (collection names starting with `_`, e.g. `_superusers`) MUST NOT generate audit rows.
- Verification scenario: After authenticating as a regular user and performing, in order, (1) create a `posts` record with `title="Original Title"`, (2) update that record changing ONLY `title` to `"Updated Title"`, (3) delete that record, the `audit_log` collection MUST contain exactly 3 new rows referencing the created post id, in this chronological order:
  1. `action=create`, `collection=posts`, `record=<posts_id>`, `actor=<user_id>`, `diff={}`
  2. `action=update`, `collection=posts`, `record=<posts_id>`, `actor=<user_id>`, `diff={"title":{"old":"Original Title","new":"Updated Title"}}` (exactly this object, no other keys)
  3. `action=delete`, `collection=posts`, `record=<posts_id>`, `actor=<user_id>`, `diff={}`
- After the scenario above, the count of `audit_log` rows where `collection="audit_log"` MUST be 0.
truth:
# Verification Plan

## Setup
1. cd /home/user/myproject
2. Build the Go binary: `go build -o app .`
3. Start the server in the background: `./app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090 &`
4. Wait until `http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/health` returns HTTP 200.
5. Pre-test cleanup using the superuser token (credentials provided via env vars `PB_SUPERUSER_EMAIL=admin@example.com`, `PB_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD=adminpass1234`): delete all existing rows in `audit_log` and any leftover rows in `posts`.

## Test Data
- Regular user: email `user@example.com`, password `testpass1234`. The id of this user is referred to as `USER_ID`.
- Superuser: email `admin@example.com`, password `adminpass1234` (used only for cleanup and final verification queries, not for the mutation scenario).

## Verification Steps
1. **Authenticate as the regular user**:
   - POST `http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/collections/users/auth-with-password` with body `{"identity":"user@example.com","password":"testpass1234"}`.
   - Expected: HTTP 200. Save `token` and `record.id` (the latter as `USER_ID`).
2. **Create a `posts` record (as the regular user)**:
   - POST `http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/collections/posts/records` with `Authorization: <token>` and body `{"title":"Original Title"}`.
   - Expected: HTTP 200/201. Save the returned `id` as `POSTS_ID`.
3. **Update the post changing only `title`**:
   - PATCH `http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/collections/posts/records/{POSTS_ID}` with `Authorization: <token>` and body `{"title":"Updated Title"}`.
   - Expected: HTTP 200.
4. **Delete the post**:
   - DELETE `http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/collections/posts/records/{POSTS_ID}` with `Authorization: <token>`.
   - Expected: HTTP 204.
5. **Authenticate as superuser to query audit log**:
   - POST `http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/collections/_superusers/auth-with-password` with body `{"identity":"admin@example.com","password":"adminpass1234"}`.
   - Save `token` for the next requests.
6. **Fetch audit rows for the post**:
   - GET `http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/collections/audit_log/records?filter=record%3D%22{POSTS_ID}%22&sort=created&perPage=200` with the superuser token.
   - Expected: exactly 3 items in this order:
     - Row 1: `action="create"`, `collection="posts"`, `record=POSTS_ID`, `actor=USER_ID`, `diff` parses to `{}`.
     - Row 2: `action="update"`, `collection="posts"`, `record=POSTS_ID`, `actor=USER_ID`, `diff` parses to exactly `{"title":{"old":"Original Title","new":"Updated Title"}}` (no other keys).
     - Row 3: `action="delete"`, `collection="posts"`, `record=POSTS_ID`, `actor=USER_ID`, `diff` parses to `{}`.
7. **Self-reference check**:
   - GET `http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/collections/audit_log/records?filter=collection%3D%22audit_log%22&perPage=1` with the superuser token.
   - Expected: `totalItems` is `0`.
